definition.en: Something that happens at a particular place and time, organised by one or more agents for a particular purpose, and is of interest to a general audience. This definition excludes natural events such as earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.

usage.en: Use this class as the common superclass for ElectionEvent. It represents something that happens at a particular place and time, organised by one or more agents for a particular purpose, and is of interest to a general audience. In the context of political advertising, this is used to describe events (such as elections or referendums) to which a political advertisement may be linked, as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

definition.en: Information describing the financial aspects of a political campaign,including sources of funding,expenditures,and financial disclosures.

usage.en: Use this class to provide aggregated financial information for a political advertising campaign, including total amount and currency, as required for transparency and reporting under EU Regulation 2024/900.

definition.en: An event in which eligible participants formally express a choice or decision to elect representatives or decide on public matters, typically through voting.

usage.en: Use this class to describe an election event that is referenced or linked to a political advertisement. When a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link to an election, the title, level, and date of the election must be disclosed in the transparency notice as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The level must be indicated as EU, national, regional, or local.

definition.en: Financial support provided for a project, activity, or entity, typically by an organisation, government, or individual.

usage.en: Use this class to represent the overall financial structure supporting a political advertisement or campaign, including both direct and indirect funding sources, as required for transparency under EU Regulation 2024/900.

definition.en: A project,program,or organised effort undertaken to achieve specific objectives, often in a political, legislative, or public context.

usage.en: Use this class to describe a legislative or regulatory initiative that is referenced or promoted by a political advertisement. When a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link to a legislative or regulatory initiative, the title and level of the initiative must be disclosed in the transparency notice under Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The title may be shortened.

definition.en: An advertisement with a political subject matter, including information about its content, format, language, publication dates,and links to legislative processes or votes.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any advertisement with political content,including its metadata and links to related legislative or voting processes.

definition.en: Information describing a political advertisement

usage.en: Use this class to detail the financial information specific to a single political advertisement, including total amount, currency, nature, and origin of funding, as required for transparency notices under EU Regulation 2024/900.

definition.en: The set of criteria, characteristics, or parameters used to define the intended audience or recipients of a political advertisement.

usage.en: Use this class to document all targeting and ad-delivery techniques based on personal data used for the political advertisement. Population of this class is mandatory when online political advertisements use such techniques, under Article 12(1)(l) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, points 1416 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The label of the political advertisement must also include a statement about the use of targeting or ad-delivery techniques under Article 11(1)(d) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

definition.en: A record providing information about a political advertisement, its funding, sponsors, targeting, and related entities, to ensure transparency and compliance with legal requirements.

usage.en: Use this class as the root element for transparency disclosures related to political advertising.

definition.en: The text data type is a combination of a string and a language identifier.

usage.en:
It is useful for names and descriptions that are available in multiple languages. Where this is so, each version of the data should be included and each one associated with the relevant language identifier. RFC 3066 provides a commonly used set of identifiers for natural languages. This is the set recognised by UN/CEFACT and XML Schema.
Languages are represented by two character codes, optionally followed by a locale definition such as "de" meaning German and "de-at" meaning "German as spoken in Austria."

definition.en: The class of literal values,eg. textual strings and integers.
anyURI represents an Internationalized Resource Identifier Reference (IRI). An anyURI value can be absolute or relative,and may have an optional fragment identifier (i.e.,it may be an IRI Reference).
The boolean datatype represents the logical values true and false.

Date represents top-open intervals of exactly one day in length on the timelines of dateTime, beginning on the beginning moment of each day, up to but not including the beginning moment of the next day).  For non-timezoned values, the top-open intervals disjointly cover the non-timezoned timeline, one per day.  For timezoned values, the intervals begin at every minute and therefore overlap.

The double datatype is patterned after the IEEE double-precision 64-bit floating point datatype [IEEE 754-2008]. Each floating point datatype has a value space that is a subset of the rational numbers. Floating point numbers are often used to approximat

Integer is ·derived· from decimal by fixing the value of ·fractionDigits· to be 0 and disallowing the trailing decimal point.  This results in the standard mathematical concept of the integer numbers.  The ·value space· of integer is the infinite set {...,-2,-1,0,1,2,...}.  The ·base type· of integer is decimal.
The string datatype represents character strings in XML.
An idea or notion; a unit of thought.
A temporal entity with zero extent or duration
A temporal interval or instant.
A self-employed person,company,or organization that has legal rights and obligations.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any legal entity involved in the context of a political advertisement, including the sponsor, the paying entity, or the legal entity ultimately controlling the sponsor. This class supports the requirements for transparency notices under EU Regulation 2024/900, which mandate disclosure of the identity, contact details, and registration information for all such entities.

definition.en: A spatial object that in a human-readable way identifies a fixed location.

usage.en: Use this class to represent a structured postal or establishment address associated with a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity in a transparency notice. Address information must include at minimum the street, number, city, postal code and country as required by Annex II, Template 3, note 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410 and Article 12(1)(a) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

definition.en: A individual human being who may be dead or alive,but not imaginary.

usage.en: The fact that a person in the context of Core Person Vocabulary cannot be imaginary makes person:Person a subclass of both foaf:Person and schema:Person which both cover imaginary characters as well as real people. The Person Class is a subclass of the more general 'Agent' class.

definition.en: Entity that is able to carry out action.

usage.en: In compliance with the description from FOAF, an Agent is considered as any entity that is able to carry out actions. The Agent class acts as a generic element which can be further specified by implementers for their usages, for example by defining the Person class from the Core Person Vocabulary or Organization from W3C's Organization Ontology as a subclasses of Agent. This Person or Organization can then issue a certain Requirement or be concerned by an Evidence provided.

definition.en: The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.

usage.en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

definition.en: The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.

usage.en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

definition.en: An electronic address through which the Contact Point can be contacted.

usage.en: Provide the email address as required for sponsors or paying entities (Article 12(1)(a)-(b)).

definition.en: An electronic address through which the Contact Point can be contacted.
A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.

usage.en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertising campaign, expressed in the specified currency. Annex II, 3.6

definition.en: A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.

usage.en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertisement, expressed in the specified currency. Annex II, 3.5

definition.en: The registered address relationship links a Resource with the legally registered Address.

usage.en: It is the address to which formal communications can be sent, such as the postal address.

definition.en: Description of the techniques used to deliver advertisements to targeted audiences.

usage.en: Describe the specific ad-delivery techniques used to disseminate the political advertisement to targeted audiences, including any use of artificial intelligence or automated systems.Annex II 3.15 a)

definition.en: Information about the analytics performed on the advertisement or its audience.

usage.en: Provide information about analytical techniques used to assess or optimise the reach and impact of the advertisement, such as audience segmentation, performance metrics, or engagement analysis.

definition.en: The method or approach used to calculate the amount or allocation of funding.

usage.en: Describe the methodology used to calculate the aggregated amounts and value of other benefits received by the providers of political advertising services, including whether the figures reflect invoiced, budgeted, and/or charged amounts and whether VAT is included. For in-kind benefits, indicate the valuation method used. This information is mandatory in the transparency notice under Article 12(1)(f) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 8 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: Financial information related to the campaign.

usage.en: Links the funding record to the aggregated financial information for the political advertising campaign, including total amounts and currency. Populate when the advertisement is part of a campaign, as required by Article 12(1)(d) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 6 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which, alone or jointly with others, determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data in the context of a targeted activity or service.

usage.en: Links the targeting record to the data controller responsible for processing personal data for targeting or ad-delivery purposes. The identity and contact details of the controller must be disclosed in the transparency notice, as required by Article 19(1)(e) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 16(a) of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: Information about the financial resources used for the political advertisement or campaign.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the funding information for the political advertisement and, where applicable, the political advertising campaign. Aggregated amounts, values of in-kind benefits, their public/private and EU/non-EU origin, and the calculation methodology are mandatory under Article 12(1)(d), (e), and (f) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, points 5-8 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: Indicates whether the origin of the funding is from the EU, from outside the EU.

usage.en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "EU", "Non-EU", or "EU and non-EU". If possible, align with official EU country groupings.

definition.en: The election related to the event

usage.en: Links the political advertisement to an election event with which it has a clear and substantial link. The title, level, and date of the election must be disclosed in the transparency notice, as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: A reference to a previous version of the same resource that was suspended or discontinued due to a regulatory or legal infringement.

usage.en: Use this association to indicate if a previous version of the advertisement was suspended or discontinued due to infringement, as required by Article 12(1)(k) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 13 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410

definition.en: The initiative related to the advertisement.

usage.en: Links the political advertisement to a legislative or regulatory initiative with which it has a clear and substantial link. The title and level of the initiative must be disclosed in the transparency notice, as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The planned date for the end of publication of the political advertisement.

usage.en: Provide the planned end date for publication or dissemination.

definition.en: The planned date for the start of publication of the political advertisement.

usage.en: Provide the planned start date for publication or dissemination.

definition.en: URL to the internal policy regarding targeting or personal data processing.

usage.en: Provide a link to the internal policy describing how targeting and ad-delivery techniques are used, as required by Article 19 of Regulation 2024/900.

definition.en: Indicates whether the political advertisement is linked to a vote.

usage.en: Indicate true if the advertisement is linked to an election event; otherwise, false.

definition.en: Indicates whether the political advertisement is linked to a legislative process.

usage.en: Indicate true if the advertisement is linked to a legislative or regulatory process; otherwise, false.

label.en: is postal address different than establishment

definition.en: Indicates whether the postal address is different from the establishment address (true) or not (false).

usage.en: Indicate true if the postal address differs from the establishment address. If true, provide the postal address.

label.en: is postal address different than establishment

definition.en: Indicates whether the postal address is different from the establishment address (true) or not (false).

usage.en: Indicate true if the postal address differs from the establishment address. If true, provide the postal address.

definition.en: A description of the political or administrative level if not covered by the controlled vocabulary.

usage.en: If the initiatives level does not fit standard categories.

definition.en: The nature of the funding (e.g., public, private, mixed).

usage.en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "Public", "Private", or "Public and private".

definition.en: Information about the mechanism for addressing previous non-compliance.

usage.en: Provide details if previousNonComplianceExists is true, describing the mechanism for addressing non-compliance.

label.en: number of clicks, likes, or comments

definition.en: The number of user interactions such as clicks, likes, or comments on the advertisement.

usage.en: State the total number of user interactions (clicks, likes, comments) with the advertisement during the dissemination period.

definition.en: The number of times the advertisement was viewed.

usage.en: State the total number of times the advertisement was displayed to users during the dissemination period.

definition.en: Any other information relevant to the targeting of the advertisement.

usage.en: Include any additional information relevant to the targeting or ad-delivery process that is not covered by other attributes.

definition.en: The entity that paid for the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the natural or legal person that provided remuneration in exchange for the political advertisement, if different from the sponsor or the entity ultimately controlling the sponsor. Mandatory when applicable, as required by Article 12(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 3 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: A paid communication intended to influence the outcome of an election or referendum, or to support or oppose a political party or candidate.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the Political Advertisement it describes. Each transparency notice must be associated with exactly one political advertisement, as required by Article 11(1)(e) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

definition.en: Financial information related to the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the funding record to the financial information specific to this individual political advertisement, including total amounts and currency. Mandatory in all transparency notices, as required by Article 12(1)(d) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 5 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.

usage.en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the initiative is being undertaken.

definition.en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.

usage.en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the election is conducted. Annex II, 3.9

definition.en: Indicates whether there has been previous non-compliance related to the political advertisement.

usage.en: Indicate true if there has been previous non-compliance related to this advertisement; otherwise, false.

definition.en: The date after which a resource or record is no longer required to be retained.

usage.en: Indicates the final date by which the transparency notice and any modifications must be retained, in accordance with applicable legal, regulatory, or organisational requirements. For political advertising transparency notices under Regulation (EU) 2024/900, this is at least seven years after the last publication of the related political advertisement. The value should be calculated as the date of last publication plus the required retention period (e.g., 7 years).

definition.en: The entity that provides financial or other support for the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the sponsor of the political advertisement. The identity of the sponsor, including name, email, postal address (where made public), and place of establishment is mandatory under Article 12(1)(a) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: Indicates the compliance status of the political advertisement, including whether it has a history of previous non-compliance.

usage.en: Use this attribute to record the compliance status of the political advertisement. If the advertisement or an earlier version has been suspended or discontinued due to an infringement, indicate this here, as required by Article 12(1)(k) and Annex II, point 13 of Regulation (EU) 2024/900

definition.en: Information about the targeting techniques and parameters used for the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the targeting and ad-delivery information. Must be populated when the political advertisement uses targeting or ad-delivery techniques based on personal data, under Article 12(1)(l) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, points 1416 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The objectives or goals of the targeting strategy.

usage.en: Describe the objectives of the targeting strategy, such as increasing engagement, reaching specific demographic groups, or influencing voting behaviour.

definition.en: The parameters or criteria used to target the advertisement.

usage.en: List the parameters or criteria used to select the audience for the advertisement (e.g., age range, location, interests).

label.en: use ad delivery techniques based on personal data

definition.en: Indicates whether ad delivery techniques are based on personal data.

usage.en: Indicate whether ad-delivery techniques for this advertisement are based on the processing of personal data (true or false).

definition.en: Indicates whether an artificial intelligence system was used in the targeting or delivery of a resource to an audience, including a description of its role and functioning.

usage.en: Indicate whether and how artificial intelligence systems were used in the targeting or ad-delivery of the political advertisement. Include a meaningful description of the AI system and its role, as required by Article 19(1)(c)(iv) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 15(e) of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: Indicates whether targeting techniques are used for this advertisement.

usage.en: Indicate whether targeting techniques were used for this advertisement (true or false).

definition.en: Link to an interface allowing the exercise of data protection rights, such as submitting requests for access, rectification, erasure, or other rights under the applicable data protection legal framework.

usage.en: Provide a URL where users can exercise their data protection rights, such as withdrawing consent or requesting data access, as required by Article 19(1)(e) of Regulation 2024/900.

definition.en: An alternative name for the resource.

usage.en: Provide the business name or any alternative or commonly used names for the legal entity.

definition.en: Date of creation of the resource.

usage.en: Provide the date the advertisement was created.

definition.en: An account of the resource.

usage.en: Include a summary or detailed account of the advertisement content, format, and context.

definition.en: The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.

usage.en: Indicate the machine-readable format (e.g., JSON, XML) per Annex III, point 2.

definition.en: A language of the resource.

usage.en: The language in which the transparency notice is written.Must match the language of the political advertisement (Art. 12(3)).

definition.en: A language of the resource.

usage.en: Provide the language in which the political advertisement is written or spoken, using a controlled vocabulary (e.g. BCP 47 language tags or ISO 639). Transparency notices must be written in the language of the political advertisement as required by Article 12(3) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

definition.en: Date on which the resource was changed.

usage.en: Record the most recent update, as required by Art. 12(3).

definition.en: A topic of the resource.

usage.en: Provide a topic or keyword describing the subject matter of the political advertisement. This helps contextualise the advertisement and supports identification of its political subject matter as required for the assessment under Article 8 of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

definition.en: A name given to the resource.

usage.en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the Initiative.

definition.en: A name given to the resource.

usage.en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the Election Event (e.g., 'parliamentary elections', 'constitutional referendum'). The title may be indicated by reference to the category of authorities being elected, as per Annex II, Template 3, note 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The name given to the Public Event.

usage.en: Provide the official name given to the public event. For elections and referendums, the title may be indicated by reference to the category of authorities being elected (e.g., 'parliamentary elections') or to a specific type (e.g., 'constitutional referendum') as per Annex II, Template 3, note 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en: Specify the type of initiative (e.g., legislative, regulatory). Annex II, 3.9

definition.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en: Specify the format or medium of the political advertisement (e.g. video, audio, display, printed, online). This determines which labelling requirements apply under Annex I of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410 (television/radio, printed media, or digital medium).

definition.en: A classification assigned to an Agent.
A string of characters to uniquely identify a concept.

usage.en: Provide a string of characters to uniquely identify the legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity.

definition.en: The beginning of a period or interval.

usage.en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination starts.

definition.en: The end of a period or interval.

usage.en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination ends.

definition.en: The association of a temporal entity (instant or interval) to any thing.

usage.en: Provide the temporal instant or interval associated with the public event. For elections and referendums, this should correspond to the date(s) of the event as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The name under which the Legal Entity is legally registered.

usage.en:
Provide the name under which the legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity is legally registered.
The legal name is different from the alternative (trade) name which is a pseudonym used by companies that do not operate under their registered company name.
A business might have more than one legal name, particularly in countries with more than one official language. In such cases, and where the encoding technology allows, the language of the string should be identified.

definition.en: An "address representation" as conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Address Representation data type. The locn:addressId property may be used to link this locn:Address to other representations.

usage.en:
For individuals, only if isAddressPublic=true; for organisations, use as the main correspondence address.

Provide the address as required for sponsors or paying entities (Article 12(1)(a)-(b)).

definition.en: A globally unique identifier for each instance of an Address.

usage.en:
The concept of adding a globally unique identifier for each instance of an address is a crucial part of the INSPIRE data spec. A number of EU countries have already implemented an ID (a UUID) in their Address Register/gazetteer, among them Denmark. OASIS xAL also includes an address identifier. It is the address Identifier that allows an address to be represented in a format other than INSPIRE whilst remaining conformant to the Core Vocabulary.

The INSPIRE method of representing addresses is very detailed, designed primarily for use in databases of addresses. Whilst data that is published in full conformance with the INSPIRE data structure can be made available using theCore Location Vocabulary the reverse is not true since the Core Vocabulary allows much greater flexibility.

Many datasets that include address data as one piece of information about something else are likely to have that data in simpler formats. These might be tailored to the specific need of the dataset, follow a national norm, or make use of a standard like vCard.

definition.en: The name of the uppermost level of the address, almost always a country.

usage.en: Provide the country of the address using a controlled vocabulary. Mandatory component of any postal or establishment address associated with a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity, as required by Annex II, Template 3, note 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Indicate the country where the initiative is taking place, using the official name or code.

definition.en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Indicate the country where the vote is taking place, using the official name or code.

definition.en: The name of a secondary level/region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.

usage.en: Provide the city or municipality of the address. Mandatory component of any postal or establishment address associated with a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity, as required by Annex II, Template 3, note 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

definition.en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

definition.en: Proper noun(s) applied to the real world entity identified by the locator. The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

usage.en:
The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

The key difference between a locator designator and a locator name is that the latter is a proper name and is unlikely to include digits. For example, "Shumann, Berlaymont" is a meeting room within the European Commission headquarters for which locator name is more appropriate than locator.

definition.en: The code created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.

usage.en: Post codes are common elements in many countries' postal address systems. One of the many post codes of Paris is for example "75000".

definition.en: A name created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.

usage.en: Usually a city, for example "Paris".

definition.en: The name of a passage or way through from one location to another.

usage.en: A thoroughfare is usually a street, but it might be a waterway or some other feature. For example, "Avenue des Champs-Élysées".

definition.en: The hereditary surname of a family.
The name(s) that identify the Person within a family with a common surname.
The website of the Public Event.

usage.en: Provide the URL of the official website of the public event. For elections or referendums linked to a political advertisement, this should link to official information about the modalities for participation, as required by Article 12(1)(h) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 10 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: A Notice for information provision associated with Consent.

usage.en: Provide a URL or reference to the notice informing users about consent for personal data processing in the context of targeting or ad delivery.

definition.en: Indicates association with Personal Data.

usage.en: Specify the categories of personal data processed for targeting or ad-delivery purposes (e.g., demographic data, browsing behaviour, political affiliation proxies). Mandatory under Article 12(1)(l) and Article 19(1)(c) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 15(c) of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

definition.en: Indicates policy applicable or used.

usage.en: Provide a link to the internal policy describing how targeting and ad-delivery techniques are used, as required by Article 19 of Regulation 2024/900.Annex II 15i

definition.en: Further information about the subject resource.

usage.en: A URL where the transparency notice can be accessed or retrieved. Provide a dereferenceable URL, ensuring easy retrieval during publication (Art. 12(3)). Annex 3.11

definition.en: Beginning of a temporal entity.

usage.en: Indicate the first date on which the targeted advertisement was disseminated or visible to users.Annex II 15f

definition.en: End of a temporal entity.

usage.en: Indicate the last date on which the targeted advertisement was disseminated or visible to users .Annex II 15f
